Back to website Python lab Docs index

Latex Symbols.Py

Clean code reading view for studying, copying, and comparing your own implementation.

emergency-path/.venv/Lib/site-packages/IPython/core/latex_symbols.py

Runs the exact Python code shown below. Output is saved in this browser.


    

    
    
    

      
# encoding: utf-8

# DO NOT EDIT THIS FILE BY HAND.

# To update this file, run the script /tools/gen_latex_symbols.py using Python 3

# This file is autogenerated from the file:
# https://raw.githubusercontent.com/JuliaLang/julia/master/stdlib/REPL/src/latex_symbols.jl
# This original list is filtered to remove any unicode characters that are not valid
# Python identifiers.

latex_symbols = {
    "\\euler": "ℯ",
    "\\ohm": "Ω",
    "\\^a": "ᵃ",
    "\\^b": "ᵇ",
    "\\^c": "ᶜ",
    "\\^d": "ᵈ",
    "\\^e": "ᵉ",
    "\\^f": "ᶠ",
    "\\^g": "ᵍ",
    "\\^h": "ʰ",
    "\\^i": "ⁱ",
    "\\^j": "ʲ",
    "\\^k": "ᵏ",
    "\\^l": "ˡ",
    "\\^m": "ᵐ",
    "\\^n": "ⁿ",
    "\\^o": "ᵒ",
    "\\^p": "ᵖ",
    "\\^r": "ʳ",
    "\\^s": "ˢ",
    "\\^t": "ᵗ",
    "\\^u": "ᵘ",
    "\\^v": "ᵛ",
    "\\^w": "ʷ",
    "\\^x": "ˣ",
    "\\^y": "ʸ",
    "\\^z": "ᶻ",

reverse_latex_symbol = {v: k for k, v in latex_symbols.items()}

No headings found.
